<mainpage-leftcolumn-start />
<!--This is the FANDOM wiki code and as such the weird stuff here is not to be treated as a normal page-->
<!--The code you see using the source editor is used to insert all the content inside the left and right columns. Please don't remove it from the Main Page because it's the optimal main page structure, and accommodates the main page ad. -->
Welcome to the '''{{SITENAME}}'''! We're a collaborative community about [[Shinto]] created by fans and for fans like you! This wiki is an online encyclopedia which [[w:Help:Editing|anyone can edit]] and contribute to, [[Special:CreatePage|creating a new page]] or [[Special:AllPages|improving an existing one]]. If you're new and don't know where to start, feel free to reach out to one of our [[Special:ListUsers/sysop|administrators]] or check out [[w:Help:Getting Started|these Fandom help pages]]!

==Important articles==
<gallery position="center" captionalign="center" navigation="true">
Itsukushima Gate.jpg|alt=A vermilion torii gate standing in the sea at Itsukushima, framed by wooded mountains.|[[Itsukushima Shrine]] — one of Japan's most iconic shrines
Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|alt=A woodblock-style depiction of the sun goddess Amaterasu emerging from a rock cave surrounded by other kami.|[[Amaterasu]] emerging from the [[Amano-Iwato]] cave
Wiki Episode.jpeg|alt=Interior view of a shrine hall with tatami flooring.|[[Jinja|Shrine architecture]] and the parts of a shrine
Wiki Mission.jpeg|alt=A shrine priest in white robes performing a purification ritual.|[[Kannushi|Priests]] and [[Matsuri|festivals]]
Wiki Location.jpeg|alt=A wooded path leading to a shrine gate.|[[Ise Grand Shrine]], [[Izumo-taisha]], and other sacred sites
Wiki Object.jpeg|alt=A bronze mirror displayed on a shrine altar.|The [[Three Sacred Treasures]] and other ritual objects
</gallery>
[[w:Help:Galleries|Galleries]] with [[w:Help:Adding images|images]] are typically used to show and link to the main important content about your topic. You can link to specific pages or [[w:Help:Categories|categories]] about them, once you have created different ones. To start creating content, you can use these buttons for each one of the content pages:

<div class="inputbox-gallery">
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Shrine page
buttonlabel=New shrine page
width=25
</inputbox>
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Kami page
buttonlabel=New kami page
width=25
</inputbox>
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Festival page
buttonlabel=New festival page
width=25
</inputbox>
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Ritual page
buttonlabel=New ritual page
width=25
</inputbox>
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Text page
buttonlabel=New text/scripture page
width=25
</inputbox>
<inputbox>
type=create
preload=Template:Sacred object page
buttonlabel=New sacred object page
width=25
</inputbox>
</div>
<!-- The gallery above works well for individual articles, but it would also be good to have another below it that points to important Category pages. -->

==Track your progress==
You can use different [[w:Help:Tables|tables]] to collect relevant information about the topic of the wiki and the related products. Be sure to add the [[w:Help:Table Progress Tracking|progress tracking feature]] where appropriate too! It allows all users to keep track of their personal progress related to missions to complete in a video game, episodes from a TV show, book chapters - whatever you can think of!

<table-progress-tracking table-id="1" unique-column-index="1">
{| class="wikitable sortable"
|-
! Shrine !! Location !! Notes
|-
| [[Ise Grand Shrine]] || Mie Prefecture || The most sacred shrine in Shinto, dedicated to [[Amaterasu]]; rebuilt every 20 years in the [[Shikinen Sengū]] tradition
|-
| [[Izumo-taisha]] || Shimane Prefecture || One of the oldest and most important shrines, dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]]
|-
| [[Itsukushima Shrine]] || Hiroshima Prefecture || Famous for its "floating" torii gate in the sea; UNESCO World Heritage Site
|-
| [[Fushimi Inari-taisha]] || Kyoto Prefecture || Head shrine of [[Inari]]; known for thousands of vermilion torii forming forest tunnels
|-
| [[Meiji Shrine]] || Tokyo || Dedicated to the deified spirits of Emperor Meiji and Empress Shōken
|-
| [[Atsuta Shrine]] || Aichi Prefecture || Houses one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures]], the sword [[Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi]]
|}
</table-progress-tracking>
<!-- You can remove this section from the Main Page and use it on different content pages at your convenience.-->
==Interactive Map==
<interactive-map name="Example map" />
[[w:Help:Interactive Maps|Interactive Maps]] are also a great way to represent the universe from the wiki topic. You can also add markers for points of interest like hidden treasures, boss locations, or quest givers, and use them to mark your progress. [[Special:AllMaps|Take a look at all the Interactive Maps on this wiki]].
<!-- Interactive Maps are usually more optimal for content pages, while Main Pages can also contain them.-->
<mainpage-endcolumn /><mainpage-rightcolumn-start />
<!-- These two lines of code you see using the source editor are also essential as they are used to close the left column and open the right column. -->
==About==
[[Shinto]] (神道, ''the way of the kami'') is the indigenous religious tradition of Japan. It centres on the veneration of [[Kami]] — sacred spirits associated with nature, ancestors, historical figures, and specific places — through rituals, festivals, and observances performed at [[Shrine|shrines]]. Shinto has no founder, no canonical scripture, and no fixed doctrine; instead it is a living tradition preserved through practice and through classical texts like the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Engishiki]].

Core starting points on this wiki:

* '''[[Shinto]]''' — the tradition itself: its history, practice, and schools
* '''[[Kami]]''' — the spirits and deities venerated in Shinto, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi]], [[Inari]], [[Hachiman]], and countless others
* '''[[Shrine]]''' / '''[[Jinja]]''' — sacred architecture, [[Torii]] gates, and famous sites like [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and [[Izumo-taisha]]
* '''[[Matsuri]]''' — the festival calendar
* '''[[Kannushi]]''', '''[[Miko]]''' — priests and shrine attendants
* '''[[Kojiki]]''', '''[[Nihon Shoki]]''', '''[[Engishiki]]''' — the foundational classical texts

<!--Alongside the introduction, using this section to describe the IP is always helpful for readers and also to improve search engine optimization (SEO).-->
==Join the conversation==
'''Discussions''' is the central meeting place where you can connect with other fans. There you can share news, chat about trending topics, introduce your personal favorite character or content, and talk about what you love most. Ready to connect? [[Special:Forum|Head over to Discussions]] and start the conversation now!

<!--As a wiki admin, don't forget to participate on Discussions and moderate what's happening there. You can also assign specific Moderators to help you there.-->
==Helping out==
''Need help building out this community?''

*[[Project:Wiki rules|Rules of this wiki]]
*[[Project:Policies|Policies of this wiki]]
*[[Project:Accessibility checklist|Accessibility checklist]]
*[[w:Help:Getting Started|Getting started]]
*[[w:Help:Contributing|How to contribute]]
*[[w:Help:Community Management|Managing your community]]
*[[w:Help:Growing your wiki|Growing your wiki]]
*[[w:Help:Contents|Guides]]
*[[w:Help:Index|All Help articles]]

You can also be part of the larger Fandom family of communities. Visit [[w:c:community|Fandom's Community Central]]!
<!-- These suggested pages are always useful. If you want to improve the Main Page with different content, don't forget to keep references for new people to help them participate on your community. -->
<mainpage-endcolumn />
[[Category:{{SITENAME}}]]
__NOTOC__
__noeditsection__
<!--The column tag you see while using the source editor is needed to close the right column. You also have to include the Main Page into a general wiki category, like the Sitename. __Notoc__ and __Noeditsection__ are used to hide the Table of Contents and the Edit pencil button in the section titles, as the Main Page has a different design from the normal content pages.-->
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
